title: potentilla palustris var. villosa
intro: Potentilla palustris var. villosa, also known as marsh cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Potentilla palustris var. villosa include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
